div.p: The uid_map and gid_map files accept lines in the following ASCII format:

ul.li: ID-INSIDE-NS: The starting UID/GID within the new namespace.
ID-OUTSIDE-NS: The starting UID/GID in the parent namespace (host).
LENGTH: The number of IDs to map in a continuous range.

div.p: Kernel enforcement: uid_map and gid_map are write-once.

ul.li: Writing to an already initialized map returns EPERM.
Mapping must be contiguous and non-overlapping.

div.p: To prevent "Group Dropping" attacks where a user drops a restrictive group, the kernel requires:

ol.li: write(setgroups, "deny") MUST occur before writing gid_map.
Failure to do so results in EPERM on the gid_map write attempt.

div.p: The child process has 0 capabilities in the host namespace, but full capabilities (e.g., CAP_NET_ADMIN, CAP_SYS_ADMIN) within the newly created namespace.
